sourceMovie.title: Wonder
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Wonder (2017) as a family theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The family and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bridge to Terabithia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Wonder compelling — the family, the drama — shows up in Bridge to Terabithia (2007) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that uplifting storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Miracles from Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The family energy of Wonder is alive in Miracles from Heaven (2016), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: A Little Princess
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Little Princess (1995) operates in the same family as Wonder, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The uplifting payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Secret Garden
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Secret Garden (2020) belongs to the same conversation as Wonder — both anchor their family in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The uplifting undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Anne of Green Gables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wonder's family stayed with you, Anne of Green Gables (1985) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Shawshank Redemption
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Wonder excels in drama, The Shawshank Redemption (1994) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Donnie Darko
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Donnie Darko (2001) is the natural next step after Wonder: same drama DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and fantasy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: American History X
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Wonder who respond to its drama will find American History X (1998) immediately familiar. Both films share a tense sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Wonder fans keep landing on The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe (2005) — the family is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Stand by Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Stand by Me draws from the same drama well as Wonder, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1986 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wonder?
faq.answer: Try Bridge to Terabithia, Miracles from Heaven, A Little Princess—each shares DNA with Wonder while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wonder?
faq.answer: Wonder (2017) is best described as Family, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wonder worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
